title: Premium corporate-website photo retouch: grade, declutter, and export a consistent responsive image set
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real Freelancer.com brief: "professionally enhance a complete set of images for our corporate website"; "Correct lighting imbalances and exposure issues"; "Enhance color, contrast, and sharpness"; "Remove distractions, unwanted objects, and background clutter"; "Create a consistent visual style across all website images"; "Resize and optimize images for web performance without compromising quality"; "Prepare images for desktop, tablet, and mobile responsiveness"; "Showcase our facilities, operations, equipment, and people in the best possible light"; "maintain a natural and realistic appearance." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable single-asset benchmark: the original posting is a vague "MUST READ BEFORE BID" open call with no images attached, so I fixed the concrete subject as a hero shot of a plant-floor engineer beside CNC/machining equipment (faithful to the Automotive/Industrial/Agriculture vertical and the "facilities, operations, equipment, and people" language) plus a paired exec portrait, and pinned the responsive export sizes (1920px desktop hero, 1024px tablet, 640px mobile) since the brief says desktop/tablet/mobile but gives no numbers. IMPORTANT capability-honest reading: the brief's literal "remove unwanted objects" is NOT done via AI object removal (forbidden / not-available). It is honored the only legitimate connector way — selecting the subject, inverting, and applying a solid neutral fill to a cluttered backdrop region plus a lens blur to suppress background clutter, which is exactly how a retoucher cleans a busy plant-floor backdrop while keeping the subject authentic. No layout composition, no generative fill, no text-to-image is claimed.
one_line_ask: Take two client-shot raw corporate images (a plant-floor engineer-by-equipment hero and an executive portrait), professionally retouch and color-grade them to one consistent premium look, declutter the busy backgrounds the allowed way, then export a web-optimized responsive set (desktop/tablet/mobile) plus a Firefly board for sign-off.
full_brief: We are launching a new corporate website for our industrial / CNC-machining business and need a Photoshop expert to professionally retouch a set of images so they look premium, modern, and consistent. Attached are two client-shot RAW-ish JPEGs straight off the camera: (1) HERO — a process engineer standing beside a CNC machining centre on the plant floor, shot under mixed fluorescent + window light so it is slightly underexposed, cool/green-cast, and the background is cluttered with parts bins, cables and a fire extinguisher; (2) a paired executive half-body portrait shot in the same facility, warmer but flat and low-contrast.\n\nWe need you to: correct the lighting imbalance and exposure on the hero; pull the white balance neutral and add real contrast, color and sharpness so it reads premium; clean up the distracting background clutter behind the engineer (we don't want a fake studio look — keep it authentic, just calm it down) by selecting the subject, inverting to a background-only region and laying a neutral fill over the clutter, then give the whole frame a gentle lens-blur soft-focus so it feels like a shallow-depth premium shot (the subject stays clean because the clutter around it was filled, not erased with AI); then carry the EXACT same finished look onto the executive portrait so the two images are visually consistent as a set. Once the look is locked, prepare web-optimized, responsive exports of the hero at desktop (1920px wide), tablet (1024px) and mobile (640px) sizes, all centred on the subject and fast-loading. Finally, collect the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops into a single review board so our marketing lead can approve the style in one click before we roll it across the rest of the site library. Keep everything natural and realistic — this is a real factory, not a render.

inputs.name: hero_engineer_cnc_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary photograph, straight-out-of-camera look, of a 40-year-old male process engineer in a navy polo and safety glasses standing beside a large CNC machining centre on a real factory floor. Slightly underexposed, mixed fluorescent-plus-window lighting giving a cool greenish color cast, flat contrast. Visible background clutter: blue plastic parts bins, coiled pneumatic cables on the wall, a red fire extinguisher, a whiteboard. Real skin pores and a few flyaway hairs, natural hands, lanyard. 3:2 landscape, 6000x4000px, no retouching, authentic industrial environment, NOT glossy, NOT CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an un-edited camera JPEG: faint green/cyan cast, lifted blacks, soft contrast, real sensor grain, genuine plant-floor clutter behind the subject so the declutter + lens-blur steps have something real to act on. Keep authentic skin texture and natural hands.

inputs.name: exec_portrait_facility_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid half-body corporate portrait, straight-out-of-camera, of a 50-year-old female executive in a charcoal blazer, photographed inside the same industrial facility with soft warm window light. Flat, low-contrast, slightly warm. Real skin texture with pores and fine lines, individual hair strands and a couple of flyaways, natural relaxed hands, soft out-of-focus shop-floor behind her. 3:2 landscape, 6000x4000px, no retouching, documentary, NOT glossy, NOT CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Same facility so the unified grade is believable, but deliberately a DIFFERENT starting tone (warm + flat) from the hero (cool + underexposed) so that applying one shared preset to both is a real consistency test, not a trivial copy.

outputs.name: hero_graded_master.png
outputs.spec: Full-resolution retouched, color-graded and preset-locked hero master (this is the hero_look_locked URN that fans out to the crops, board and zip): exposure/WB corrected, contrast+color+sharpness enhanced, cluttered backdrop region calmed with a solid neutral fill (subject preserved by the inverted subject mask), a whole-image lens-blur soft-focus finishing pass, then the shared 'corporate premium' preset applied. ~6000x4000px PNG, the locked premium look.

outputs.name: portrait_graded_master.png
outputs.spec: Executive portrait carrying the IDENTICAL finished look (same preset) as the hero so the pair reads as one consistent set. ~6000x4000px PNG.

outputs.name: hero_desktop_1920.png
outputs.spec: Web-optimized desktop hero crop centred on the subject, 1920px wide (16:9), from the graded master.

outputs.name: hero_tablet_1024.png
outputs.spec: Responsive tablet crop, 1024px wide, subject-centred, from the graded master.

outputs.name: hero_mobile_640.png
outputs.spec: Responsive mobile crop, 640px wide, subject-centred, fast-loading, from the graded master.

outputs.name: corporate_retouch_review_board
outputs.spec: Firefly Board deep-link assembling the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops for one-click marketing sign-off.

outputs.name: corporate_website_image_set.zip
outputs.spec: Single ZIP bundling the two graded masters and the three responsive crops for handoff to the web team.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize the upload of the client's raw hero image (hero_engineer_cnc_raw.jpg) into Adobe storage to get a presigned target.
Finalize the upload, yielding a stored, addressable hero raw asset URN used by every downstream edit.
First pass at the lighting imbalance: auto-tone the underexposed hero to recover a sane global exposure/contrast baseline before targeted fixes.
Pull the cool/green mixed-lighting cast back to neutral white balance ('correct lighting imbalances').
Add real contrast and punch so the flat factory shot reads premium ('enhance contrast and sharpness').
Enhance color while protecting skin tones ('enhance color'), completing the tonal grade on the hero.
Select the engineer so the background can be treated without touching the subject (foundation for the declutter).
Invert to a background-only mask covering the cluttered plant-floor backdrop.

SOLID-FILL only: lay a neutral mid-gray over the cluttered backdrop region (defined by the inverted background-only mask) to calm the distracting bins/cables/extinguisher the allowed way (NOT AI object removal), keeping the masked subject untouched — this masked solid fill is what keeps the subject clean while the surrounding clutter is neutralised.

Whole-image lens-blur soft-focus finishing pass over the already-decluttered frame (this connector applies to the whole image, not a masked region) to give the graded shot a shallow-depth, premium feel. The subject already reads cleanly because the surrounding clutter was solid-filled in step 9, not because the blur is masked.
List available Lightroom presets to choose the single shared 'corporate premium' look that will lock cross-image consistency.

Apply the chosen preset to the graded+decluttered+soft-focused hero to define the canonical finished look and produce hero_graded_master, the deliverable that fans out to the crops, board and zip (the exact same preset is reused on the portrait for consistency).
Initialize upload of the second client asset, the executive portrait (exec_portrait_facility_raw.jpg).
Finalize the portrait upload into a stored asset URN.

Apply the SAME preset chosen at step 12 to the warm/flat portrait so it matches the hero — the 'consistent visual style across all images' requirement, and a real test since the portrait started from a different tone.
Subject-centred web-optimized desktop crop of the graded hero master at 1920px wide (16:9).
Responsive tablet export at 1024px wide from the same graded hero master.
Responsive mobile export at 640px wide, subject-centred and fast-loading, completing desktop/tablet/mobile coverage.
Assemble the graded hero master, matching portrait and three responsive crops into one Firefly Board for one-click marketing sign-off (presents assets already produced; does not generate art).
Single unavoidable local packaging step: zip the two masters and three responsive crops for handoff to the web team.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. The two client assets enter via upload (1→2 hero, 13→14 portrait); nothing else is loaded from outside. The hero grade is a linear pipeline where each step consumes the prior output: hero_raw_asset → auto_tone(3) → color_temperature(4) → brightness_contrast(5) → vibrance_saturation(6). The declutter branch reuses hero_color: select_subject(7) → invert_selection(8) → fill_area(9, consuming hero_color + the inverted mask; a masked SOLID FILL that keeps the subject clean) → lens_blur(10, a WHOLE-IMAGE soft-focus finishing pass, no mask) = hero_lens_soft. list_presets(11) feeds apply_preset(12) which consumes hero_lens_soft to produce hero_graded_master — the single canonical deliverable — AND emits preset_catalog, the consistency linchpin: the SAME preset_catalog choice is reused on portrait_raw_asset at step 15 so both images share one look. hero_graded_master then fans out to three responsive crops (16,17,18). Finally all five rendered deliverables (hero_graded_master, portrait_graded_master, and the three crops) converge into the Firefly board (19) and the local zip (20). Every step after the two initial loads consumes a named prior output via inputs_from; the premium responsive deliverable set emerges purely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 20 steps, 14 distinct Adobe tools (well above the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool floor), spanning asset I/O, a multi-stage tonal/color grade, mask-based selective editing, preset-driven cross-image consistency, responsive multi-size export, and board assembly. Difficulty comes from (a) the multi-branch dependency graph — a linear grade, a select→invert→fill→blur declutter branch reusing an intermediate, and a preset that must be captured once and reused on a second, differently-toned image; (b) capability discipline: the brief literally says 'remove unwanted objects', which a naive agent would attempt via forbidden AI object-removal — the honest solution routes it through select_subject→invert→solid fill_area→lens_blur, the only allowed path; (c) consistency is a genuine test because the portrait starts warm/flat while the hero starts cool/underexposed, so a shared preset must actually unify two different starting points; (d) the 1920/1024/640 responsive fan-out and final convergence into a single board + zip. All connector work is [C] headless-confirmed with a single permitted [L] zip at the end.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capabilities used. image_fill_area is a masked SOLID neutral fill of the cluttered backdrop region (not AI object-removal, outpaint, or reframe-crop). image_apply_lens_blur is correctly declared and described as WHOLE-IMAGE (inputs_from is only hero_bg_calmed, no mask); the prior 'subject sharp via selective blur' overclaim is removed. No generative expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo. All crops via image_crop_and_resize (gen-free, never larger than the 6000x4000 source). Every input a step consumes is declared. All actions are real Adobe connector tools plus one permitted local zip.
reverify.notes: Both flagged descriptive/naming slips are resolved: (1) step-10 note and the hero_graded_master.png spec no longer imply a background-selective/subject-preserving blur — the subject stays clean because of the step-9 masked solid fill, and the lens blur is stated as whole-image soft-focus. (2) Naming is reconciled: the preset-locked master that actually fans out to crops/board/zip is now named hero_graded_master (matching the declared output), so the declared deliverable name and the delivered URN align. Renamed the pre-preset intermediate to hero_lens_soft to remove the collision. Chain remains coherent and fully connector-doable.
